paragraph: The single-family market in Maywood is moving briskly. With a median sale price of $715,000 and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.048, homes that are priced right are selling above asking, and the median days on market sits at 13 days. Over the last seven months, 22 single-family homes sold, with 91 percent of them going for more than their list price. That's competitive but not frenzied. Sellers aren't dictating terms the way they do in truly hot markets, but they're also not desperate to move fast. If your home is in good condition and realistically priced, you'll draw attention. The range of sales speaks to a fairly diverse inventory. Homes are selling anywhere from $505,000 to $885,000, so there's room for different buyer profiles and different neighborhoods within town. Inventory is small, which means less choice for buyers, but it also means less time on market for sellers who know what they have.
